description_long: This temple features a grand, multi-tiered pyramidal structure with a series of receding platforms, each adorned with smaller spires and intricate architectural details. The central spire rises prominently above the rest, giving the building a striking vertical emphasis. The exterior appears to be constructed from dark stone, with a weathered, matte finish that suggests age and durability. The base is broad and solid, with symmetrical stairways and angular buttresses flanking the main entrance. Decorative elements are densely packed, especially on the upper tiers, with repeating geometric and possibly floral motifs. The overall form and ornamentation evoke the grandeur of Southeast Asian or South Asian temple architecture, reminiscent of structures like Borobudur or Angkor Wat.
description: A large, dark stone temple with a multi-tiered pyramidal structure, crowned by a central spire and dense decorative motifs. The base is broad and solid, with symmetrical stairways and angular buttresses.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance, flanked by angular buttresses and a prominent stairway leading up to the first tier.
description_view_1: This view is nearly identical to view 0, showing the symmetrical layout and the central spire rising above the receding tiers.
description_view_2: Again, the same frontal perspective is visible, emphasizing the layered platforms and dense ornamentation on the upper levels.
description_view_3: This view matches the previous ones, with the central spire and detailed tiered structure clearly visible.
attribute_ornamentation: dense geometric and floral carvings, repeating motifs on upper tiers, minimal figurative elements
attribute_entrance_design: rectangular main entrance with a broad stairway, flanked by angular buttresses and framed by stepped architectural elements, minimal ornamentation at the base
